## 9121307a-3a0d-4cd4-a30a-2184ad39afec
This server helps users set up and manage their Shopify stores.

## Store Creation
Users who don't have a store yet can use get-new-store-previews to create one from a short description.
After calling get-new-store-previews, encourage the user to browse the previews and pick the theme they like best.
Each preview has a signup link — signing up through it creates a real Shopify store with that theme and matching products pre-installed.
Encourage continued brainstorming: users can try different descriptions, tweak ideas, or explore new directions without commitment.

## Store Management
Once a store exists, this server provides tools for managing it:
- Products: search, create, update, bulk-update status, and organize into collections.
- Collections: search, create, update, and manage product groupings.
- Orders: list recent orders and get detailed order information.
- Customers: list and search customers.
- Inventory: check inventory levels and set quantities at specific locations.
- Analytics: run ShopifyQL queries for sales, order, and product performance data.
- Discounts: create percentage-based discount codes.
- Images: upload images to Shopify's CDN for use in products.

## GraphQL — General-Purpose Access
The Shopify Admin API exposes hundreds of resources. The built-in tools above are optimized shortcuts for common operations (with structured output and widgets), but they only cover a subset of the API.
When the user asks about a resource that has no dedicated built-in tool — such as gift cards, metafields, metaobjects, pages, blogs, markets, translations, publications, and many others — use graphql_query (for reads) or graphql_mutation (for writes) to fulfill the request. NEVER tell the user that data is unavailable just because there is no built-in tool for it.

Decision logic:
1. If a built-in tool matches the request, prefer it (better UX).
2. If no built-in tool exists, use graphql_query or graphql_mutation.

## Shopify Developer Tools
These tools help you construct correct GraphQL operations. They do

- make: Run the project Makefile (regenerates Xcode project via xcodegen and opens it).
- post-merge: Sync main after a PR has been merged (git checkout main + pull --ff-only + make).
- pr: Wrap current uncommitted changes into a Feature/RAD-XXXX branch and open a Bitbucket PR.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- pdf-viewer:annotate: Collaboratively annotate a PDF — propose markup, review together, iterate
- pdf-viewer:fill-form: Fill PDF form fields interactively with live visual feedback
- pdf-viewer:open: Open a PDF in the interactive viewer
- pdf-viewer:sign: Place a signature or initials image on a PDF
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:extract-design: Extract the full design language from any website URL. Produces 8 output files including AI-optimized markdown, visual HTML preview, Tailwind config, React theme, shadcn/ui theme, Figma variables, W3C design tokens, and CSS variables. Also runs WCAG accessibility scoring. Use when user says 'extract design', 'get design system', 'design language', 'design tokens', 'what colors/fonts does this site use', or '/extract-design'.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- pdf-viewer:view-pdf: Interactive PDF viewer. Use when the user wants to open, show, or view a PDF and collaborate on it visually — annotate, highlight, stamp, fill form fields, place signature/initials, or review markup together. Not for summarization or text extraction (use native Read instead).
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	//
2	//  RadiationLevelChartViewModel.swift
3	//  Radiacode
4	//
5	//  Created by Matvey Kozin on 23.01.2024.
6	//
7	
8	import Foundation
9	import Combine
10	
11	@Observable
12	final class RadiationLevelViewModel {
13	    
14	    enum Consts {
15	        static let rawDataMaxCount = 600
16	    }
17	    
18	    var averagingRawData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel] = []
19	    var purifiedData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel] = []
20	    
21	    var value: Float?
22	    var error: Float?
23	    var level: SignalizationAlarmLevel = .normal
24	
25	    private let queue: DispatchQueue
26	    private let chartModelBuilder: ChartModelBuilder
27	
28	    @ObservationIgnored
29	    private var fullRawData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel] = []
30	    @ObservationIgnored
31	    private var cancellable: Set<AnyCancellable> = .init()
32	    
33	    init(
34	        chartModelBuilder: ChartModelBuilder,
35	        rawDataPublisher: AnyPublisher<DataBufferBatch, Never>,
36	        purifiedDataPublisher: AnyPublisher<DataBufferBatch, Never>,
37	        valuePublisher: AnyPublisher<RadiationData, Never>,
38	        initialDataPublisher: AnyPublisher<[DataBufferBatch], Never>,
39	        queueLabel: String
40	    ) {
41	        self.queue = DispatchQueue(label: "com.radiacode.\(queueLabel).buffer")
42	        self.chartModelBuilder = chartModelBuilder
43	        
44	        rawDataPublisher
45	            .map { RadiationLevelChartDataModel(count: $0.value, time: $0.time) }
46	            .receive(on: queue)
47	            .sink { [weak self] rawData in
48	                guard let self else { return }
49	                fullRawData.insertSorted(rawData)
50	                if fullRawData.count > Consts.rawDataMaxCount {
51	                    fullRawData.removeFirst(fullRawData.count - Consts.rawDataMaxCount)
52	                }
53	                let averagingData = chartModelBuilder.averagingRawData(rawData: fullRawData)
54	                Task { @MainActor in
55	                    self.averagingRawData = averagingData
56	                }
57	            }
58	            .store(in: &cancellable)
59	        
60	        purifiedDataPublisher
61	            .map { RadiationLevelChartDataModel(count: $0.value, time: $0.time) }

20	        rawData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel],

24	    ) -> LineChartView {

26	        let rawDataSet = TimeOrderedLineDataSet(label: Consts.rawDataSetLabel)
27	        rawDataSet.axisDependency = .right

33	
34	        rawDataSet.drawCirclesEnabled = false

37	        rawDataSet.setDrawHighlightIndicators(false)

44	        purifiedDataSet.setColor(color)
45	        purifiedDataSet.lineWidth = 1
46	        purifiedDataSet.fillAlpha = 0.25

49	        purifiedDataSet.drawCirclesEnabled = false
50	        purifiedDataSet.drawValuesEnabled = false
51	        purifiedDataSet.highlightEnabled = false

59	        chartView.highlightPerDragEnabled = false
60	        chartView.doubleTapToZoomEnabled = false
61	        chartView.leftAxis.enabled = false
62	        chartView.legend.enabled = false
63	        chartView.autoScaleMinMaxEnabled = true
64	        chartView.minOffset = 0
65	        
66	        return chartView
67	    }

69	    static func makeRadiationLevelChart(
70	        rawData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel],
71	        purifiedData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel],
72	        timeRange: RadiationLevelOptions.TimeRange,
73	        scale: RadiationLevelOptions.Scale,
74	        color: NSUIColor,
75	        coordinator: RadiationLevelChartCoordinator,
76	        indexes: Binding<[Int]>,
77	        mainSourceForCalculating: Bool,
78	        radiationType: RadiationType.Level?
79	    ) -> LineChartView {
80	        let chartView = makeChartView(
81	            rawData: rawData,
82	            purifiedData: purifiedData,
83	            lineWidth: 1,
84	            color: color
85	        )
86	        chartView.delegate = coordinator
87	
88	        chartView.pinchZoomEnabled = true
89	        chartView.dragXEnabled = true
90	        
91	        chartView.xAxis.labelTextColor = UIColor(.textSecondary)
92	        chartView.xAxis.labelPosition = .bottom
93	        chartView.xAxis.valueFormatter = DateAxisValueFormatter()
94	        chartView.xAxis.gridLineDashPhase = 4.0
95	        chartView.xAxis.gridLineDashLengths = [4.0]
96	        chartView.xAxis.axisLineColor = .clear
97	        chartView.xAxis.gridColor = UIColor(.separatorFill)
98	        chartView.xAxis.labelCount = 4
99	        chartView.xAxis.drawGridLinesBehindDataEnabled = true
100	
101	        chartView.rightAxis.valueFormatter = PrecisionAxisValueFormatter(radiationType: radiationType)
102	        chartView.rightAxis.axisMinimum = 0
103	
104	        chartView.rightAxis.axisLineColor = .clear
105	        chartView.rightAxis.labelTextColor = UIColor(.textSecondary)
106	        chartView.rightAxis.labelPosition = .insideChart
107	        chartView.rightAxis.labelXOffset = 32
108	        chartView.extraRightOffset = 32
109	        chartView.rightAxis.gridColor = UIColor(.separatorFill)
110	        
111	        chartView.renderer = RadiationLevelChartRenderer(
112	            dataProvider: chartView,
113	            animator: chartView.chartAnimator,
114	            viewPortHandler: chartView.viewPortHandler,
115	            indexes: indexes,
116	            mainSourceForCalculating: mainSourceForCalculating
117	        )
118	
119	        chartView.legendRenderer = NoOpLegendRenderer(
120	            viewPortHandler: chartView.viewPortHandler,
121	            legend: chartView.legend
122	        )
123	
124	        chartView.leftYAxisRenderer = NoOpYAxisRenderer(
125	            viewPortHandler: chartView.viewPortHandler,
126	            axis: chartView.leftAxis,
127	            transformer: chartView.getTransformer(forAxis: .left)
128	        )
129	        
130	        switch scale {
131	        case .initial:
132	            if let data = chartView.data {
133	                chartView.focusX(minXValue: data.xMax - timeRange.timeInterval, maxXValue: data.xMax)
134	            }
135	        case let .live(minX, maxX),
136	            let .position(minX, maxX):
137	            chartView.focusX(minXValue: minX, maxXValue: maxX)
138	        }
139	        
140	        Task { @MainActor in
141	            chartView.notifyDataSetChanged()
142	        }
143	        
144	        coordinator.cachedPurifiedData = purifiedData
145	        coordinator.cachedRawData = rawData
146	        coordinator.cachedTimeRange = timeRange
147	        coordinator.cachedScale = scale
148	        
149	        return chartView
150	    }

153	    func updateData(
154	        rawData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel],
155	        purifiedData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel],
156	        coordinator: RadiationLevelCoordinatorProtocol
157	    ) -> Bool {
158	        guard
159	            let rawDataSet = data?.dataSets.first(where: { $0.label == Consts.rawDataSetLabel }) as? LineChartDataSet,
160	            let purifiedDataSet = data?.dataSets.first(where: { $0.label == Consts.purifiedDataSetLabel }) as? LineChartDataSet
161	        else {
162	            return false
163	        }
164	       
165	        var changed = false
166	
167	        if rawDataSet.count > RadiationLevelViewModel.Consts.rawDataMaxCount {
168	            rawDataSet.removeFirst(rawDataSet.count - RadiationLevelViewModel.Consts.rawDataMaxCount)
169	            changed = true
170	        }
171	        
172	        if rawData != coordinator.cachedRawData {
173	            if rawData.isEmpty {
174	                rawDataSet.removeAll(keepingCapacity: true)
175	            } else {
176	                let excludeArray = Set(coordinator.cachedRawData)
177	                let addedObjects = rawData.filter { !excludeArray.contains($0) }
178	                for addedObject in addedObjects {
179	                    let chartDataEntry: ChartDataEntry = .init(x: addedObject.time, y: Double(addedObject.count))
180	                    _ = rawDataSet.addEntryOrdered(chartDataEntry)
181	                }
182	            }
183	            coordinator.cachedRawData = rawData
184	            changed = true
185	        }
186	        
187	        if purifiedData != coordinator.cachedPurifiedData {
188	            if purifiedData.isEmpty {
189	                purifiedDataSet.removeAll(keepingCapacity: true)
190	            } else {
191	                let excludeArray = Set(coordinator.cachedPurifiedData)
192	                let addedObjects = purifiedData.filter { !excludeArray.contains($0) }
193	                for addedObject in addedObjects {
194	                    let chartDataEntry: ChartDataEntry = .init(x: addedObject.time, y: Double(addedObject.count))
195	                    _ = purifiedDataSet.addEntryOrdered(chartDataEntry)
196	                }
197	            }
198	            coordinator.cachedPurifiedData = purifiedData
199	            changed = true
200	        }
201	        
202	        return changed
203	    }

content.text: Нашёл: DGCharts 5.1.0. В `drawValues:556` вызывается `_xBounds.set`, который через `ChartDataSet.entryIndex` делает **линейный** `firstIndex(of:)` (O(n)) с `ChartDataEntry.isEqual` на каждом кадре. Теперь проверю размер датасета и наш `TimeOrderedLineDataSet` — растёт ли `purifiedData` без лимита.

`TimeOrderedLineDataSet` уже переопределяет `entryIndex` бинарным поиском — но стек показывает вызов **базового** `ChartDataSet.entryIndex` (линейный `firstIndex(of:)`), а не нашего. Подозрение: сигнатура override не совпадает. Проверю базовый метод в DGCharts.

//
//  ShareSheet.swift
//  Radiacode
//
//  Created by Matvey Kozin on 27.12.2023.
//

import SwiftUI

struct ShareSheet: UIViewControllerRepresentable {

    typealias Callback = (
        _ activityType: UIActivity.ActivityType?,
        _ completed: Bool,
        _ returnedItems: [Any]?,
        _ error: Error?
    ) -> Void
    
    var activityItems: [Any]
    var applicationActivities: [UIActivity]?

    var callback: Callback?
    
    func makeUIViewController(context: Context) -> UIActivityViewController {
        let controller = UIActivityViewController(
            activityItems: activityItems,
            applicationActivities: applicationActivities
        )
        controller.excludedActivityTypes = ShareSheet.excludedActivityTypes
        controller.completionWithItemsHandler = { activity, success, items, error in
            self.callback?(activity, success, items, error)
            if success {
                controller.dismiss(animated: true)
            }
        }

        return controller
    }
    
    func updateUIViewController(_ uiViewController: UIActivityViewController, context: Context) {}
}

#Preview {
    ShareSheet(activityItems: ["A preview string" as NSString])
}

private extension ShareSheet {
    static let excludedActivityTypes: [UIActivity.ActivityType] = [
        .print,
        .openInIBooks,
        .copyToPasteboard,
        .addToReadingList,

content.text: `ShareSheet` оборачивает `UIActivityViewController`, но **не задаёт** `popoverPresentationController`. content.text: Все 11 мест используют один `ShareSheet` внутри `.sheet {}`. Посмотрю фактическую презентацию в паре мест, чтобы понять минимальный фикс.

Разобрался. Issue **[RADIACODE-IOS-1V](https://radiacode.sentry.io/issues/RADIACODE-IOS-1V)**.

## Что упало

`NSGenericException: UIActivityViewController can only be used modally or as contentViewController in popover on iPad.` — фатальный, 1 раз, 1 пользователь, релиз `1.0.16+6` (текущий), iPhone 15 Pro Max, iOS 18.7.7.

Хвост стека — суть:
```
-[UIActivityViewController viewDidAppear:]
-[SHSheetPresenter activityViewControllerDidAppear:]
+[NSException raise:format:]
```

## Где в коде

Шаринг сделан через [ShareSheet.swift](RadiaCode/Presentation/Design System/Views/ShareSheet.swift) — `UIViewControllerRepresentable` поверх `UIActivityViewController`, и он встраивается как **контент SwiftUI `.sheet`** (11 мест, напр. [SpectrumMainView.swift:137](RadiaCode/Presentation/Containers/Spectrum/SpectrumMainView.swift:137), [TrackListView.swift:109](RadiaCode/Presentation/Containers/Track/List/TrackListView.swift:109)):

```swift
.sheet(isPresented: $shareSpectrum) {
    ShareSheet(activityItems: [spectrumXML])
        .presentationDetents([.medium])
}
```

При таком встраивании `UIActivityViewController` не презентован ни модально, ни как popover — он является `contentViewController` чужого sheet. На **compact** size class (обычный iPhone в портрете) UIKit это терпит; на **regular** — в `viewDidAppear` бросает это исключение.

## Почему на iPhone, а не iPad

`iPhone16,2` = iPhone 15 Pro Max. У Max/Plus в **ландшафте** `horizontalSizeClass == .regular` — и UIKit начинает требовать popover-презентацию, как на iPad. То есть пользователь, скорее всего, открыл share и повернул телефон в ландшафт (либо был в ландшафте). Тот же краш ловится на iPad и на «Designed for iPad» на Mac. Точную ориентацию Sentry не отдаёт, но механизм однозначен: `UIActivityViewController` как контент `.sheet` на regular size class.

## Фикс

`ShareLink` (нативный путь) тут **не подходит** — он не поддерживает ни `excludedActivityTypes` (а список большой), ни `completionWithItemsHandler` (используется в `TrackListView`/`SpectrogramLibraryView` для `success`).

Правильный путь — **презентовать `UIActivityViewController` императивно** (а не как контент `.sheet`) и задать `popoverPresentationController` (sourceView + центр, `permittedArrowDirections = []`). Делается одним SwiftUI-модификатором, тогда сам `UIActivityViewController` со всеми excluded/callback сохраняется:

```swift
// невидимый презентер в .background(...)
func updateUIViewController(_ vc: UIViewController, context: Context) {
    guard isPresented, vc.presentedViewController == nil else { return }
    let controller = UIActivityViewController(activityItems: activityItems, applicationActivities: nil)
    controller.excludedActivityTypes = ShareSheet.excludedActivityTypes
    controller.completionWithItemsHandler = { activity, success, items, error in
        callback?(activity, success, items, error)
        isPresented = false
    }
    if let popover = controller.popoverPresentationController {            // ← на regular это и спасает
        popover.sourceView = vc.view
        popover.sourceRect = CGRect(x: vc.view.bounds.midX, y: vc.view.bounds.midY, width: 0, height: 0)
        popover.permittedArrowDirections = []
    }
    vc.present(controller, animated: true)
}
```

Использование меняется с `.sheet { ShareSheet(...) }` на `.shareSheet(isPresented:/item:)` в тех же 11 местах.

Объём: новый модификатор (`ShareSheet.swift`) + механическая правка 11 вызовов. Логику excluded-типов и колбэков сохраняем.

Хочешь — соберу полный diff (новый модификатор + все 11 мест) и покажу. Делать буду только по слову **применить**. Если предпочитаешь другой подход (например, оставить `.sheet`, но презентовать поверх через контейнер) — скажи.
Дай проверю пару фактов о таргете (ориентации, idiom, deployment target) — от них зависит, какие варианты вообще реалистичны. Не хочу гадать.

content.text:
Сначала уточнение к моему прошлому объяснению — проверил таргет:

- iPhone: `UISupportedInterfaceOrientations` = **только Portrait**.
- iPad: landscape + portrait (приложение универсальное, `TARGETED_DEVICE_FAMILY` включает iPad).
- Deployment target: **iOS 17**.

Значит на iPhone ландшафта быть не может — моя гипотеза «Max в landscape» **неверна**. Первопричина та же (UIActivityViewController встроен как контент чужого `.sheet`, а не презентован сам modally/в popover), но конкретный триггер на этом iPhone иной — вероятнее всего взаимодействие `.sheet` + `.presentationDetents([.medium])` со встроенным `UIActivityViewController`. Точную комбинацию по 1 событию воспроизвести нельзя; важно, что на iPad этот код крашится гарантированно. Любой из вариантов ниже закрывает все триггеры сразу.

## Варианты фикса

**1. Невидимый презентер + императивный `present` с popover** (мой прошлый Вариант B)
`UIActivityViewController` презентуется сам, задаётся `popoverPresentationController`. Меняется `ShareSheet.swift` + 11 мест (`.sheet{…}` → `.shareSheet(…)`).
＋ канонично, надёжно на iPhone/iPad/Mac; сохраняем excluded+callback. − трогаем 11 мест.

**2. Сервис-презентер через активную сцену**
Хелпер берёт topmost VC из foreground `UIWindowScene` и презентует share. Вызов из действия кнопки, без `.sheet` и без representable.
＋ просто, без SwiftUI-обёрток. − не «SwiftUI-way»; аккуратность с несколькими сценами/таймингом; тоже правим места вызова.

**3. Контейнер-VC внутри `.sheet`**
`ShareSheet` возвращает прозрачный контейнер, который в `viewDidAppear` сам презентует `UIActivityViewController` и закрывается по завершении.
＋ правит как будто один файл. − на деле всё равно надо убирать `.presentationDetents([.medium])` в местах вызова (иначе мелькает серая подложка) → те же 11 мест + UX-артефакт. Не рекомендую.

**4. `.popover` вместо `.sheet`**
На regular `.popover` даёт popover-презентацию (валидно — «contentViewController in popover»), на compact адаптируется в sheet.
＋ маленькая правка. − поведение адаптации/detents хрупкое, особенно с medium-detent; нужен `sourceRect`; риск регрессий на разных iOS.

**5. Гибрид с `ShareLink`**
Где не нужны excluded/callback — нативный `ShareLink` (iOS 17 ок); где нужны — representable с фиксом.
＋ часть кода становится нативной. − теряем единый excluded-список там, где перейдём на `ShareLink` (вылезут «мусорные» расширения); непоследовательно.

**6. Ограничить ориентацию / `requiresFullScreen`** — не фикс: на iPhone уже portrait (краш всё равно случился), на iPad нельзя убирать landscape без потери функциональности, и это не снимает требование popover. Только как временный митигатор для iPad, не решение.

## Рекомендация

**Вариант 1** — единственный, что надёжно и единообразно закрывает iPhone + iPad + Mac, сохраняя `excludedActivityTypes` и `completionWithItemsHandler`. Объём — механическая правка 11 вызовов на новый модификатор.

Скажи, какой оформлять (по умолчанию — 1) — соберу полный diff и покажу. Применю только по слову **применить**.
Понял — нужен хотфикс с минимальным диффом. Проверю, насколько вызовы единообразны (есть ли везде `.presentationDetents`), чтобы понять, можно ли обойтись правкой только `ShareSheet.swift`.

content.content:
Web search results for query: "UIActivityViewController SwiftUI sheet presentationDetents crash "can only be used modally or as contentViewController in popover""

Links: [{"title":"AppActivityView crashing when not shown as .sheet() · Issue #342 · SwiftUIX/SwiftUIX","url":"https://github.com/SwiftUIX/SwiftUIX/issues/342"},{"title":"How to present UIActivityViewController on iPhone and iPad | by Dushyant Bansal | Medium","url":"https://dushyant37.medium.com/how-to-present-uiactivityviewcontroller-on-iphone-and-ipad-ae72013d2a5a"},{"title":"SwiftUI Sheets: Modal, Bottom, and full screen in iOS","url":"https://www.swiftyplace.com/blog/swiftui-sheets-modals-bottom-sheets-fullscreen-presentation-in-ios"},{"title":"ActionSheet crash on iPad | Apple Developer Forums","url":"https://developer.apple.com/forums/thread/124530"},{"title":"[Solution] SwiftUI ActionSheet crash on iPad | by Liem Vo | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@liemvo/solution-swiftui-actionsheet-crash-on-ipad-f0a5b3a7f755"}]

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about this issue.

The error "UIActivityViewController can only be used modally or as contentViewController in popover on iPad" occurs when trying to present UIActivityViewController using methods other than .sheet().

The core issue is that UIActivityViewController is presented in a popover on iPad, and on iPad, you must present the view controller in a popover. Additionally, you must provide location information for this popover through the view controller's popoverPresentationController, providing either a sourceView and sourceRect or a barButtonItem.

The problem with using `presentationDetents` and sheet modifiers with `UIActivityViewController` is that this approach conflicts with UIActivityViewController's requirement to be presented modally on iPhone or as a popover with proper location information on iPad. One suggested workaround is using the newer iOS 15 sheet APIs to create a half-height modal, though this may not directly resolve the UIActivityViewController constraint issue.

The most reliable approach is to use `.sheet()` for iPhone presentations while ensuring proper popover configuration on iPad when necessary.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Веб-поиск (источники ниже) подтверждает фундаментальную вещь, без гадания:

- `UIActivityViewController` **не предназначен** для встраивания как произвольный контент; на iPhone его обычно нормально показывать через `.sheet()`, а на iPad он обязан быть в popover с `sourceView/sourceRect`.
- Кастомизация презентации (`presentationDetents` и пр.) **конфликтует** с этим требованием — это наиболее вероятный триггер именно нашего краша на iPhone.

Отсюда честный вывод по твоему выбору (остаёмся в `.sheet`/SwiftUI, без ручного `present`):

**Гарантированно убрать краш на iPad, не выходя за рамки `.sheet`, нельзя** — там обязателен popover, а это уже UIKit-презентация, которую ты не хочешь. Но самый вероятный триггер нашего *реального* краша (iPhone) — `.presentationDetents([.medium])` поверх share-листа. Его можно снять минимальной правкой, оставаясь полностью в `.sheet`.

## Минимальная правка в рамках `.sheet`

Во всех 10 местах убрать у `ShareSheet` модификаторы `.ignoresSafeArea()` и `.presentationDetents([.medium])` — `.sheet` остаётся, `ShareSheet` остаётся, подход не меняется:

```diff
 .sheet(isPresented: $shareSpectrum) {
     ShareSheet(activityItems: [spectrumXML])
-        .ignoresSafeArea()
-        .presentationDetents([.medium])
 }
```
(тот же паттерн в `MigrationErrorView`, `TracksMainOptionsView`, `TrackListView`, `TrackPreviewView`, `DatabaseOptionsView`, `SpectrogramMainView`, `SpectrogramLibraryView`, `SpectrumMainView`, `SpectrumLibraryView`, `SpectrumPreviewView`). `ShareSheet.swift` не трогаем.

Честно про ожидания: это снимает **вероятную** причину краша на iPhone (наш единственный кейс — 1 событие), но 100% гарантии не даёт, и на iPad/Mac код остаётся хрупким. Полную гарантию дают только: popover-конфигурация (UIKit-`present`, ты отверг) либо `ShareLink` (теряем `excludedActivityTypes` и колбэк `editMode`).

Хочешь — внесу эту минимальную правку (по слову **применить**). Либо, если для тебя важнее именно надёжность, можем пересмотреть `ShareLink`/popover, несмотря на их минусы.

Sources:
- [AppActivityView crashing when not shown as .sheet() — SwiftUIX #342](https://github.com/SwiftUIX/SwiftUIX/issues/342)
- [How to present UIActivityViewController on iPhone and iPad — Dushyant Bansal](https://dushyant37.medium.com/how-to-present-uiactivityviewcontroller-on-iphone-and-ipad-ae72013d2a5a)
- [SwiftUI ActionSheet crash on iPad — Liem Vo](https://medium.com/@liemvo/solution-swiftui-actionsheet-crash-on-ipad-f0a5b3a7f755)

Текущие незакоммиченные изменения — это согласованный фикс гонки в виджете (тот самый первый вопрос сессии). Как раз RAD-1188.

**RAD-1188** — «Убрать состояние гонки с Виджета» (статус: *В работе*)

`git diff HEAD --stat`:
```
WidgetLiveActivityManager.swift | 7 ++++---
project.yml                     | 2 +-
2 files changed, 5 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)
```

Что в diff:
- **`WidgetLiveActivityManager.swift`** — фикс гонки в нашем согласованном виде: `getRadiationData()` помечен `@MainActor`, вызовы стали `await getRadiationData()` в `startNewLiveActivity`/`updateActivity`, а `stopLiveActivity` использует `Task { @MainActor in … }`. Точно соответствует тикету.
- **`project.yml`** — `CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION: 6 → 7` (бамп номера билда). Не часть фикса гонки, но при `git add -u` попадёт в этот же коммит.

Коммитим как `RAD-1188 Убрать состояние гонки с Виджета`?

Уточни заодно по `project.yml`: включаем бамп версии в этот PR или коммитим только `WidgetLiveActivityManager.swift`?

Принято — коммитим оба файла. «да» на гейт получено, дальше по флоу без вопросов.

Создаю ветку и готовлю проект к сборке.
